topic: The Graduate AI Coding Workflow: Prototype in Bolt/Lovable, Ship in Cursor/Claude Code

key_points: Compares Cursor, Bolt, and Lovable side-by-side for different use cases
Positions Lovable as best for non-technical founders prototyping full-stack apps
Highlights the 'graduate workflow': start in Lovable, move to Cursor for production
Covers GitHub integration to bridge both tools
Compares Cursor, Claude Code, Windsurf, VS Code, Lovable, and Bolt
Frames tools on a spectrum from 'vibe coding' to 'professional agentic coding'
Explains how prototyping tools differ from production-grade AI code editors
Useful for readers deciding where to start on the spectrum
Honest review: Lovable handles first 80% of an MVP quickly
Recommends graduating to Cursor or Claude Code for serious production work
Highlights Lovable's strength in full-stack generation from a single prompt
Points out limitations when apps grow in complexity
Practical guide to multi-tool workflow: Claude, Cursor, v0, Lovable together
Suggests starting with Claude Projects for PRD, then prototyping in Bolt/Lovable
Shows how to feed PRD context into multiple tools for consistent results
Covers the handoff step between prototype and production tools
Real developer workflow: Claude Projects + Bolt + VS Code
Demonstrates a GitHub-based handoff without requiring Cursor
Advocates cloning to standard tools to avoid proprietary lock-in
Shows how to maintain context continuity across tools via PRD

stats.fact: Cursor surpassed $2B in annualized revenue by February 2026, making it the fastest-scaling B2B software company on record.
Lovable hit $400M ARR in February 2026, representing 2,800% year-over-year growth from $7M ARR at end of 2024.
Bolt.new achieved $40M ARR in just 4.5 months, reaching 5M+ users, adding over 1M new users per month.
84% of developers are using or planning to use AI tools in their development process.
Developers complete coding tasks 55% faster using GitHub Copilot (1h 11min vs 2h 41min without).
The no-code AI platform market is projected to grow from $8.6B in 2026 to $75.14B by 2034 (31.13% CAGR).
Citizen developers already outnumber professional software developers four to one.
Claude Code scores 80.8% on SWE-bench Verified — the highest publicly reported score among available tools.
Lovable has approximately 8 million users and surpassed 25 million total projects as of early 2026.
A JetBrains January 2026 survey found 18% of developers use Cursor at work; Claude Code also sits at 18%, behind GitHub Copilot at 29%.

angles: The 'graduate workflow' as a named, repeatable pattern: prototype fast → validate → graduate to production-grade tools
Bolt/Lovable handle the first 80% of an MVP; Cursor/Claude Code handle the last 20% and ongoing production
The GitHub handoff as the key integration point — same repo, two tools, continuous sync
Non-technical founders vs. developers: different entry points, same destination
Cost optimization: free-tier stacking across Lovable, Bolt, and GitHub before committing to Cursor/Claude Code subscriptions
Claude Code's agentic advantage for production: full codebase context, multi-agent orchestration, CI/CD integration
When to graduate: signals that you've outgrown a browser-based builder (complex state, multi-tenant, auth edge cases)
The hidden cost of staying in Bolt/Lovable too long: technical debt that accumulates when prototypes scale
keywords: bolt lovable cursor claude code workflow
ai app builder vs ai code editor
when to switch from lovable to cursor
outline: H2: The Two Phases of Modern AI Development (Prototype vs. Production)
H2: Phase 1 — Rapid Prototyping with Bolt.new and Lovable
H2: Phase 2 — Graduating to Production with Cursor and Claude Code
H2: The GitHub Handoff: How to Bridge Prototype and Production
H2: When to Graduate — 5 Signals You've Outgrown Your Prototype Tool
H2: Tool Costs and Free-Tier Strategy
H2: Real-World Workflow: From Idea to Shipped in One Week
